mnemonics.title: COP - Class Object Properties
mnemonics.content: Class = Object + Properties/Methods. A Class is a blueprint that defines an Object with its data (properties) and functions (methods).

mnemonics.title: C.O.P. - Constructor Builds, Destructor Demolishes
mnemonics.content: Constructor (ctor) = Creates/initializer the object when it's born. Destructor (dtor) = Cleans up when object is destroyed. Remember: 'C' for Create, 'D' for Delete.

mnemonics.title: P.P.P. - Public Pokes Through, Private Protects, Protected Preserves
mnemonics.content: Public: accessible to all (poke through anywhere). Private: accessible only within class (protected from outside). Protected: accessible within class and derived classes (preserved for family).

mnemonics.title: G.S. - Get and Set
mnemonics.content: Getter methods retrieve private data (get what you want), Setter methods modify private data (set what you want). Used for controlled access to private members.

acronyms.usage: Programming paradigm based on objects (classes as blueprints). Use when discussing the programming style C++ follows.
Original name for C++. C++ = C language + Classes. Use to remember that C++ adds OOP features to C.
Special member function with same name as class, called automatically when object is created. Use when initializing object data.
Special member function with tilde (~) before class name, called automatically when object is destroyed. Use for cleanup operations.
Three access specifiers in C++. Remember this order - most open to most restricted access.
A class creates a custom data type. Use when referring to classes as custom types you create.

visualTips.visualization: Picture an architect's blueprint for a house. The blueprint itself is the Class - it defines structure, rooms, doors, windows but isn't a real house yet. All houses built from this one blueprint are Objects.
Think of a cookie cutter (Class) making multiple cookies (Objects). Each cookie is an instance - same shape/size (structure) but individual (separate memory/location).

visualTips.concept: Access Modifiers as Walls with Doors
visualTips.visualization: Imagine a house (Class): Public = walls with many doors (everyone enters). Private = walls with no doors (no one enters). Protected = walls with doors only for family (derived classes). The data is inside, doors control access.
Visualize data (variables) wrapped inside a protective capsule (class) with only controlled openings (public methods) to interact with the outside world - like medicine in a capsule.
If Class = Living Being, then member functions = what it CAN DO (eat, sleep, walk). Data members = what it HAS (name, age, color). Both are part of the same object.

analogies.analogy: Class = Recipe for a cake. Object = The actual cake made using that recipe. Many cakes (objects) can be made from one recipe (class), each occupying space on a plate (memory).
Like bank vault money (private data) - only bank tellers (public methods) can access it on your behalf. You don't let customers go directly to the vault.
Like the birth process - when a baby (object) is born, certain initial setups happen (naming, weight, height). Constructor does the same for new objects.
Like funeral/cleanup after someone dies - resources are released, final goodbyes happen. Destructor cleans up when object lifetime ends.
Like a library book checkout system. You can't directly access library books (private data), but librarians (getter/setter methods) help you borrow (get) or return (set) books properly with rules.
Like a family inheritance - only family members (derived classes) can access certain family secrets (protected members), outsiders (other classes) cannot.
